E1001.title: Invalid escape sequence
E1001.explanation: A backslash in a string literal is followed by an invalid character. Valid escapes: \", \\, \n, \t, \r, \0, \xHH.
E1001.fix: Use a valid escape sequence. Remove or replace the bad escape.

E1002.title: Unterminated string literal
E1002.explanation: The lexer reached end-of-input before finding a closing double-quote.
E1002.fix: Add the closing " to terminate the string literal.

E1003.title: Character outside permitted character set
E1003.explanation: A byte that is not whitespace, alphanumeric, or a recognized symbol was found. toke is ASCII only. Uppercase letters and underscores in identifiers also trigger this.
E1003.fix: Remove or replace non-ASCII characters. Use lowercase identifiers without underscores.

E1010.title: Reserved literal used as identifier
E1010.explanation: true and false cannot be used as variable or function names.

E2001.title: Declaration ordering violation
E2001.explanation: Source file must follow order: m (module), i (import), t (type), c (constant), f (function). Also fires when m= is missing.
E2001.fix: Add m=name; as the first line. Ensure declarations appear in the required order.

E2002.title: Unexpected token
E2002.explanation: The parser found a token it cannot consume in the current context. Common causes: commas instead of semicolons, wrong keywords (fn instead of f=, for instead of lp, else instead of el, return instead of <).
E2002.fix: Check for commas (use semicolons instead), misplaced operators, or non-toke keywords near the reported position.

E2003.title: Missing semicolon
E2003.explanation: A semicolon is required between consecutive statements inside a block. Semicolons may be omitted only before } or at EOF.

E2004.title: Unclosed delimiter
E2004.explanation: A ( { or [ was opened but the matching closing delimiter was not found.

E2010.title: Pointer type outside extern function
E2010.explanation: Pointer types (*T) are only valid in extern (bodyless) function signatures.
E2010.fix: Remove the pointer type or make the function an extern declaration (remove the body).

E2030.title: Unresolved import
E2030.explanation: The module path in an i= declaration does not resolve to any .tki interface file.
E2030.fix: Check module path spelling. Common stdlib modules: std.io, std.str, std.math, std.file, std.json, std.time, std.http.

E2031.title: Circular import detected
E2031.explanation: The import graph contains a cycle.
E2031.fix: Restructure modules to break the circular dependency.

E2035.title: Malformed version string in import
E2035.explanation: The optional version string does not match MAJOR.MINOR or MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH format.
E2035.fix: Use a valid version string like "1.0" or remove the version specifier.

E2036.title: No compatible version found
E2036.explanation: Interface files were found but none match the requested version constraint.
E2036.fix: Update the version constraint or use the available version.

E2037.title: Version conflict between imports
E2037.explanation: Two imports reference the same module with different major versions.
E2037.fix: Align all imports of the same module to the same major version.

E3011.title: Identifier not declared
E3011.explanation: A reference to an identifier that does not exist in any enclosing scope.
E3011.fix: Declare the identifier before use, check for typos, or check that the import alias matches (e.g. i=s:std.str then use s.len not str.len).

E3012.title: Identifier already declared in this scope
E3012.explanation: A second declaration of the same name in the same scope. Shadowing across scope boundaries is allowed but duplicate in one scope is not.
E3012.fix: Use a different name for the second binding, or remove the duplicate.

E3020.title: ! applied to non-error-union value
E3020.explanation: The propagation operator ! can only be applied to a value with error union type (T!Err), inside a function that also returns an error union.
E3020.fix: Ensure the expression has an error union type and the enclosing function returns an error union.

E4010.title: Non-exhaustive match
E4010.explanation: A match expression must cover all variants of the scrutinee type.
E4010.fix: Add the missing match arm(s) to cover all cases.

E4011.title: Match arms have inconsistent types
E4011.explanation: All arms of a match expression must evaluate to the same type.
E4011.fix: Ensure all arms return the same type.

E4025.title: Struct has no field with that name
E4025.explanation: A field access (expr.field) references a field name that does not exist in the struct.
E4025.fix: Check the struct type declaration for correct field names.

E4031.title: Type mismatch
E4031.explanation: Mismatched types in binary expression, function argument, binding annotation, assignment, or return value.
E4031.fix: Ensure both sides have matching types. Use 'as' for explicit casts (e.g. x as i64).

E4040.title: Map key type mismatch
E4040.explanation: All map keys must have the same type.
E4040.fix: Ensure all keys in the map literal have the same type.

E4041.title: Map value type mismatch
E4041.explanation: All map values must have the same type.
E4041.fix: Ensure all values in the map literal have the same type.

E4042.title: Method on non-collection type
E4042.explanation: Collection methods (.push, .get, .len) called on a non-collection type.
E4042.fix: Ensure the receiver is an array or map type.

E4043.title: Inconsistent types in map literal
E4043.explanation: All entries in a map literal must have the same key type and same value type. The first entry sets the expected types.
E4043.fix: Ensure all map keys have the same type and all map values have the same type.

E4050.title: spawn argument not a callable function
E4050.explanation: spawn(f) requires its argument to be a declared function.
E4050.fix: Pass a function name (not an expression) to spawn.

E4051.title: await argument not a Task
E4051.explanation: await(t) requires its argument to have type Task<T>.
E4051.fix: Only call await on values returned by spawn.

E4052.title: Spawned function has parameters
E4052.fix: Wrap the parameterized function in a nullary function.

E4060.title: FFI type mismatch
E4060.fix: Use compatible types for FFI function signatures.

E4070.title: Assignment to immutable binding
E4070.explanation: Attempting to reassign a variable declared with plain 'let x=val' (immutable). Only variables declared with 'let x=mut.val' can be reassigned.
E4070.fix: Change 'let x=val' to 'let x=mut.val' for any variable that needs reassignment. EVERY variable that gets x=x+1 or x=newval MUST use mut.

E5001.title: Value escapes arena scope
E5001.explanation: Inside an {arena ...} block, assigning an arena-allocated value to a variable declared in an outer scope creates a dangling reference.
E5001.fix: Keep arena-allocated values within the arena block. Only write computed non-pointer results to outer mutable bindings.

W1010.title: String interpolation not yet supported
W1010.explanation: The \( sequence inside a string literal is reserved for future string interpolation.
W1010.fix: Use str.concat() or str.buf()/str.add()/str.done() for string composition instead.
